workflow.name: Extract and qualify local business leads and draft cold emails with OpenAI, Apify and Hunter
workflow.description:
# AI-Powered Business Lead Scraping, Qualification & Outreach System

## Description
**Search → Scrape → Qualify → CRM → Email Draft Automation**

**Categories:** Lead Generation, Sales Automation, AI Enrichment, Revenue Ops

---

This workflow automatically finds local businesses, extracts real contact details from their websites, qualifies them, and writes everything into a CRM — with personalized cold email drafts ready to send.

It’s designed to remove the manual grind from lead sourcing and first-touch outreach.

**Search → leads → context → drafts → done.**

---

## What This Workflow Does

This automation takes raw local business results and turns them into usable, qualified leads:

- Pulls local business websites from a search dataset  
- Scrapes each site for real contact information  
- Normalizes emails, phones, names, and addresses  
- Qualifies leads based on reachability and ops signals  
- Writes clean, deduplicated records into a CRM  
- Generates human-sounding cold email drafts  

No copying websites. No guessing emails. No messy spreadsheets.

---

## Why This Exists

Most lead gen systems fail before outreach even starts.

They rely on:
- Shallow scraped data  
- Guessy enrichment  
- Low-quality lists  
- Manual cleanup  

This system fixes that by grounding everything in what actually exists on the business website, then using AI only where it makes sense.

**Human judgment at the edges. Automation in the middle.**

---

## How It Works (High Level)

### 1. Lead Source Ingestion (Apify Dataset)

The workflow starts with a dataset of local business search results.

This can be:
- Google search results  
- Industry-specific directories  
- Any Apify-powered source that includes URLs  

Batch size is intentionally limited for safety.

---

### 2. Website Scraping

Each business website is fetched and stripped down to raw text.

Failures are allowed — broken sites simply don’t qualify later.

The raw content becomes the single source of truth.

---

### 3. AI Contact Extraction & Normalization

AI parses the site content to extract:
- Emails and phones  
- Company name and address  
- Contact people and titles  
- Social links and contact pages  
- Context snippets for traceability  

Everything is normalized and returned as strict JSON.  
If something isn’t clearly present, it stays empty.

---

### 4. Lead Qualification

Leads are scored based on:
- Reachability (email + website)  
- Basic operational signals  
- Optional social presence  

Low-quality or unreachable leads are filtered out automatically.

---

### 5. CRM Write (Google Sheets)

Qualified leads are written into a lightweight CRM:

- Append-or-update by email  
- Safe to re-run  
- Easy to inspect and debug  

This sheet becomes the system of record.

---

### 6. Cold Email Draft Generation

For each qualified lead, AI generates a personalized cold email draft:

- Casual, human tone  
- Uses real site context  
- Stored as drafts only  
- Never auto-sent  

Perfect for review, sequencing, or export into an outreach tool.

---

## Tools Used

- **n8n** — workflow orchestration  
- **Apify** — lead sourcing  
- **OpenAI** — extraction, qualification, email drafting  
- **Google Sheets** — lightweight CRM  
- **Hunter** — email verification  
- **Tavily** — optional enrichment & validation  

---

## Who This Is For

- Automation and AI agencies  
- Consultants doing outbound  
- Freelancers selling repeatable services  
- Local-service lead gen operators  
- Anyone tired of low-quality scraped lists  

---

## Customization Notes

- Swap Google Sheets for Airtable, HubSpot, or Notion  
- Adjust qualification thresholds to control lead volume  
- Replace Apify source with any directory or search dataset  
- Plug drafts into any outbound sequencing tool  
- Extend metadata for analytics or CRM sync  

---

## Difficulty & Cost

**Difficulty:** Intermediate  
(Simple concept, careful execution)

**Estimated setup time:** 30–45 minutes  

**Ongoing cost:**  
OpenAI + Apify + verification APIs only

---

## Summary

This is not just a scraper.

It’s a **lead intelligence pipeline** that turns raw search results into real, usable outbound opportunities.

**Search → scrape → qualify → CRM → drafts**  
No guessing. No junk leads. No manual cleanup.

workflow.name: AI-Powered Local Business Lead Scraping, Qualification and Outreach System

values.content:
You will receive stripped HTML / site text (raw content with tags removed or simplified). Extract real contact details, normalize them, and return ONLY a JSON object that validates against the schema below.

If a field is missing, leave it as an empty string "" (or empty array [] if the schema says array). Never guess. Never invent. No null. No "N/A". No comments. No extra keys.

Priority order:
1) emails
2) phone numbers (convert to E.164 only when you can infer country reliably)
3) everything else that helps reach them: names/titles, company name, address, contact page URL, socials, messengers, hours, generic inboxes
4) one single-line icebreaker for outreach

Be strict:
- Only include items explicitly present in the text, including common obfuscations like "name [at] domain dot com" (de-obfuscate conservatively).
- Emails: lowercase + trim; must include @ and a valid TLD; ignore obvious junk (image filenames, example.com, lorem).
- Phones: keep phone_raw; E.164 only if country is clear from context (country name/code/format). If unclear, keep e164 as "".
- URLs: keep as seen; include scheme if present.
- Names/titles: human-readable; don't guess gender.
- Addresses: single line, keep as written; don't mix hours/directions into it.
- Deduplicate case-insensitively; keep the clearest variant.
- confidence_overall is a heuristic 0–1; reserve 0.95+ for unambiguous labeled contact info.
- For each extracted item, include a short context snippet (<=140 chars) in the corresponding *_context field.

If you are unsure about an item, leave it out.

Icebreaker:
After filling the schema, set icebreaker_line to exactly one polite sentence in this format:
"Hi—I'm not sure if this is {name} or perhaps the front office, but I'm a big fan of {paraphrasedApproach} and wanted to run something by {him/her}."
- {name} = person_name if present, else company_legal_name
- {paraphrasedApproach} = 3–8 words describing what they do
- {him/her} = only if clearly inferable; otherwise use "them"
Return one single line.

Return format: ONLY the JSON object. No preamble. No markdown.

⸻

JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) — Single-Item Contact

{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "title": "ContactExtractionSingle",
  "type": "object",
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "required": [
    "source_url",
    "company_legal_name",
    "email",
    "email_context",
    "phone_raw",
    "phone_e164",
    "phone_type",
    "phone_label",
    "phone_context",
    "social_platform",
    "social_url",
    "social_context",
    "messenger_platform",
    "messenger_handle",
    "messenger_context",
    "contact_page_url",
    "contact_page_context",
    "address_line",
    "address_label",
    "address_context",
    "person_name",
    "person_title",
    "person_email",
    "person_phone_raw",
    "person_context",
    "notes",
    "confidence_overall",
    "icebreaker_line"
  ],
  "properties": {
    "source_url": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "company_legal_name": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "email": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "email_context": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "phone_raw": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "phone_e164": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "phone_type": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["main", "mobile", "toll_free", "fax", "sales", "support", "billing", "other"],
      "default": "other"
    },
    "phone_label": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "phone_context": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "social_platform": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["facebook", "instagram", "linkedin", "twitter", "x", "youtube", "tiktok", "github", "glassdoor", "other", ""],
      "default": ""
    },
    "social_url": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "social_context": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "messenger_platform": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["whatsapp", "telegram", "wechat", "line", "signal", "facebook_messenger", "other", ""],
      "default": ""
    },
    "messenger_handle": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "messenger_context": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "contact_page_url": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "contact_page_context": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "address_line": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "address_label": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "address_context": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "person_name": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "person_title": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "person_email": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "person_phone_raw": { "type": "string", "default": "" },
    "person_context": { "type": "string", "default": "" },

    "notes": { "type": "string", "maxLength": 180, "default": "" },
    "confidence_overall": { "type": "number", "minimum": 0, "maximum": 1 },

    "icebreaker_line": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "One-line icebreaker email of the format: 'Hi—I'm not sure if this is {name} or perhaps the front office, but I'm a big fan of {paraphrasedApproach} and wanted to run something by {him/her}.'",
      "default": ""
    }
  }
}

You’re personalizing a cold email based on a prospect’s LinkedIn profile.

You must fill 5 parts: subjectLine, icebreaker, elevatorPitch, callToAction, ps.
Use the templates below as the base, but make them feel human:
- Paraphrase. Don’t copy LinkedIn text.
- Keep it short, casual, “bar conversation”. No fancy language.
- Subject line MUST be non-capitalized.

Templates:
Subject Line: "hey {name}, think I have something for you re: {thingAboutThem}"
Icebreaker: "I know you're doing {thing} and I've been following {relatedThing} for a while so I figured it made sense to chat"
Elevator Pitch: "The TLDR: I think I can add ~$5K/mo to {theirParaphrasedBusiness} with a few automated systems"
Call to Action: "I just did this for a very similar {industry} company and we hit $28,350 in a few months—they do {relatedThing}—so am very confident I can duplicate this at minimum. Would be 100% risk-free, I'd guarantee at least 20 appts booked or you wouldn't pay."
PS: "even if we just chat I'd love to hear about what you're doing with {thing}"

Return ONLY valid JSON in exactly this shape (no extra keys, no prose):
{"email_address":"","subjectLine":"","icebreaker":"","elevatorPitch":"","callToAction":"","ps":""}

parameters.content:
# AI-Powered Local Business Lead Scraping, Qualification & Outreach System

What this workflow does (end-to-end)
This workflow automatically:
	1.	Pulls local business leads from Apify (Google Search / SERP data)
	2.	Scrapes each business website
	3.	Extracts & normalizes contact info using AI
	4.	Classifies lead quality & ops needs
	5.	Verifies email deliverability
	6.	Writes qualified leads into a CRM (Google Sheets)
	7.	Generates personalized cold emails for outreach

This is a modular, production-ready lead pipeline, you can disable or swap components without breaking the rest.

## ⚙️ Required Setup (DO THIS FIRST)
Accounts / Credentials you must connect
	•	Apify (Dataset access)
	•	OpenAI (for normalization + email writing)
	•	Google Sheets (CRM destination)
	•	Hunter (email verification)
	•	Tavily (optional enrichment / validation)

Before running
	1.	Replace Apify Dataset ID with your own
	2.	Update Google Sheet ID + Sheet Name
	3.	Confirm OpenAI model access (default: gpt-5-mini)
	4.	(Optional) Adjust quality thresholds in Filter Qualified Leads

How to run
	•	Manual trigger → Execute workflow
	•	Start small (Limit node is enabled intentionally)
	•	Increase batch size only after validation

## Lead Source (Apify)

This section pulls raw local business results from Apify.
Everything downstream assumes each item has a website URL.

If you want different industries or cities, change it in Apify, not here.
The Limit node is intentional — don’t remove it until you trust the pipeline.

## Website Scraping

This section fetches the business homepage and raw site content.

Some sites will fail or block scraping. That’s expected.
Failures won’t stop the workflow, they just won’t qualify later.

The raw text is passed directly into AI for flexible parsing.

values.content:
You are an experienced AI automation agency expert.
You apply real-world conversion experience only to interpret explicit signals.
Do not infer or assume missing information.
If uncertain, default to false.
Output only valid JSON.

1. Classification (3 category in total)
Determine Lead Ops Needs (boolean), can be any of the below:
    •   needs_lead_centralization
	•	needs_follow_up_automation
	•	needs_booking_automation
Strong evidence examples:
	•	Multiple inbound channels (form + email + phone + social)
	•	No clear booking flow
	•	Generic contact inbox
	•	No response-time or follow-up expectations stated

Determine Email AND website (bool)

Determine other social (linkedin, X, facebook etc) (bool)
⸻

Quality tiers
Not qualified  (0)
	•	No email AND website
→ You can’t reliably reach them

⸻

Acceptable Quality (1)

Has email and site

Reachable, but fragile or manual.

⸻
Med Quality (2)

Has email and site and Any one of Ops need Or other social
	
⸻

High Quality (3)

Has all 3 cate


{
  "has_email and phone": false,
  "need lead op": false,
  "has social: false,
  "quality_tier": "0 | 1 | 2 | 3"
}



1. Classification (3 categories total)

Determine the following three categories.

⸻

A. Lead Ops Needs (boolean)

Set to true if any of the following lead operations needs are evident:
	•	needs_lead_centralization
	•	needs_follow_up_automation
	•	needs_booking_automation

Strong evidence examples:
	•	Multiple inbound channels (form + email + phone + social)
	•	No clear booking or scheduling flow
	•	Generic contact inbox (e.g. info@, contact@)
	•	No stated response-time or follow-up expectations

⸻

B. Email AND Website (boolean)

Determine whether both an email address and a website are present.

⸻

C. Other Social Presence (boolean)

Determine whether any social presence is listed
(e.g. LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Facebook, Instagram).

⸻

Quality Tiers

0 — Not Qualified
	•	No email and website
→ The business cannot be reliably reached.

⸻

1 — Acceptable Quality
	•	Has email and website

⸻

2 — Medium Quality
	•	Has email and website
	•	AND any one of:
	•	Lead ops need
	•	Social presence

⸻

3 — High Quality
	•	Has all three categories:
	•	Email + website
	•	Lead ops need
	•	Social presence

output schema:
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "has_email_and_site": {
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "has_lead_ops_need": {
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "has_social_presence": {
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "quality_tier": {
      "type": "integer",
      "enum": [0, 1, 2, 3]
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "has_email_and_site",
    "has_lead_ops_need",
    "has_social_presence",
    "quality_tier",
    "email (if exists)"
  ],
  "additionalProperties": false
}

parameters.content:
## Qualification & Validation

This section decides whether a lead is worth keeping.

It checks:
	•	Reachability (email + site)
	•	Basic ops signals
	•	Social presence (if any)

Low-quality leads are filtered out before hitting the CRM.
If you want more or fewer leads, adjust the filter — not the AI prompt.

## CRM Write

This is the system of record.

Qualified leads are written to Google Sheets using append-or-update logic.
Re-running the workflow won’t duplicate rows if the email already exists.

If you’re debugging, always inspect the sheet first.

## Cold Email Drafting

This agent writes human-sounding cold email drafts using the extracted context.

These are drafts only.
They are stored in the CRM for review, sequencing, or export.

Do not auto-send unless you add your own approval layer.

data.details: The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
